sourceMovie.title: Dangal
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Dangal (2016) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Encanto
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dangal clicked for you, Encanto (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Karate Kid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dangal clicked for you, The Karate Kid (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dangal?
faq.answer: Try Encanto, The Karate Kid, The Rookie—each shares DNA with Dangal while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dangal?
faq.answer: Dangal (2016) is best described as Drama, Family, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dangal worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
